title: Add several podman push tests
body:
Add five tests for podman push tests:
- push to docker with authorization
- push to docker-archive
- push to docker-daemon
- push to oci-archive
- push to ostree

comments.body: @ypu Awesome work.

@ypu TYVM for these tests.  It's really very nicely done.  If possible in a later submit, it would be nice to NOT do the podman login in new tests and verify that the creds and auth parameters work appropriately.  Then if you're really energetic, similar tests for pull and build too.

LGTM assuming happy tests.
One more thought.  Tests toggling --tls-verify to be true/false would also be a nice add.

@ypu nice work ... one thing that is currently giving us fits is the communication between docker and fedora registries can be problematic ... we had begun entertaining the idea that we should run our own registry for the tests specifically and populate it with the the images we use (busybox, alpine, fedoraMinimal), but it could also be used for push's as well.

you do not need to implement this but I wonder what you think about the idea and whether this is something you would consider doing ?
@TomSweeneyRedHat Thanks for your advice. Will work on it and update the code.

@baude I also met the problem of communication to registries, especially I am in China and sometimes the network is very poor here.  It should be great to have a stable fast registry for testing.  This problem depends on lots of things, not sure just use another one will resolve it. But our own registry will help a lot to simplify the test steps for push, pull, login and logout related test cases.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 2eb38a0) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@ypu Need a rebase.  We can merge these tests in and then work on other tests in different PRs

Hi @rhatdan 
pull req already updated, but some tests still failed(not the added ones), is there any command can help to rerun the tests?
bot, retest this please

Hi
I updated this pull request and add two functions in libpod_suite_test.go: WaitContainerReady and IsCommandAvailable. This help to make  the test more stable. Can you help to review it again? Thanks a lot.
Getting some bizarre test failures. I'll rerun tests to see if they're consistent
bot, retest this please
Failures seem consistent - issues opening /dev/null?

Hi @mheon

Run the test in my host. And use lsof in another console to check the open files in the system. It shows that there are always some open files left. That will cause the open files number keep increase. As the open files belongs to e2e.test, so it will be released only after the test is finished.

Here is some output from the lsof command:
```
# lsof |grep e2e |grep deleted
e2e.test  25301             root    5u      REG              253,0        64    5573559 /tmp/podman_test630697429/crio/storage.lock (deleted)
e2e.test  25301             root    6u      REG              253,0        64   10028956 /tmp/podman_test630697429/crio/vfs-layers/layers.lock (deleted)
e2e.test  25301             root    7u      REG              253,0        64   14029283 /tmp/podman_test630697429/crio/vfs-images/images.lock (deleted)
e2e.test  25301             root    8u      REG              253,0         0     866937 /tmp/podman_test630697429/crio/vfs-containers/containers.lock (deleted)
e2e.test  25301             root    9u      REG              253,0        64   10028957 /tmp/podman_test038931566/crio/storage.lock (deleted)
e2e.test  25301             root   10u      REG              253,0        64   14029285 /tmp/podman_test038931566/crio/vfs-layers/layers.lock (deleted)
e2e.test  25301             root   11u      REG              253,0        64     866939 /tmp/podman_test038931566/crio/vfs-images/images.lock (deleted)
...
```
Interesting - sounds like we're not closing our storage.Store in some cases. I'll look into this more tomorrow.

@ypu Bit of an update - spent some time trying to chase this down today. It looks like the file leak is coming from the test suite itself. Not sure exactly what's going on yet, but it's not specific to your PR.
@ypu Found the problem. #612 should fix it. Can you please rebase once it's merged to pick up the fix to the test suite?

@ypu Update on this - we have the cause, but don't have a solution yet. I'm pushing a temporary fix in #615 that should stop the failing tests while we figure out how to solve the leaking files problem.
@mheon Thanks for your update, and now the tests are all passed. Thanks again for your help.
